reviews.title: This watch was a bust for me
reviews.text:
PROS

1. The silicone band is very comfortable. Apparently if it breaks, it can be replaced, though I’m not sure where or how you would buy a new band.

2. The appearance is sleek and modern, and the size of the watch face makes the digital numbers easy to read.

3. It has a rechargeable battery, which may have a longer life than the non-rechargeable batteries of most sports watches.

4. If you want a smart watch that is far cheaper than most brands, this might work for you.

CONS

1. There are three primary functions that I use a sports watch for: to instantly check the time, a stopwatch, and a timer. It irritated me no end with this watch that, after only a few seconds of being lit up, its face goes dark. While this might save the charge on the rechargeable battery, there are plenty of times when my right hand it not free to punch the button on this watch to light up its face, and I can’t quickly and conveniently check the time! It is also a hassle trying to use the stopwatch and timer functions.

2. I absolutely loathe the way this watch’s battery is recharged. Instead of using a standard C cable, the charging cable attaches magnetically, and it becomes detached far too easily. There is also no warning when the battery of this watch is about to go dead.

3. I don’t like the software this phone uses. It is a major hassle to learn for what I personally consider very little useful return. Why? Because most of the functions offered by this watch that are of any interest to me I can get through my smartphone. I use my phone and a Bluetooth for phone calls, texting, music, and hearing alarms. I check the weather by looking around me when outside or looking at weather apps on my phone. I use my phone for photography.
The rest of the functions this watch offers I personally find to be useless. I do not need a watch to tell me how to do a deep breathing exercises. As a healthy individual with no heart issues, I do not need to monitor my heart rate 24/7. (And if I did, I would use medical equipment that is far more reliable than this cheap watch.) I don’t need to obsessively monitor my sleep. (And, again, if I did, I wouldn’t trust this cheap watch to do an accurate job of it.)

4. Its pedometer function might have been of use to me, but unfortunately, it is inaccurate.

CONCLUSION

I dumped this watch and went back to a regular, inexpensive sports watch.
reviews.author: AHW7ETGBKGX6DCG2LBAG5YAXU46A

reviews.title: Fun
reviews.text:
The kids liked their watch. It was good. They really liked that it felt like a smart watch. The different games and other activities on it were fun for them to do. They really could do a lot with it. It had a lot of the different things you would think a smart watch would have. I also thought it could be educational.

It worked just fine. We had no problems with it. It was pretty easy for them to learn and understand how to use. It was cute on them. Obviously it is not small, but it was easy for them to wear. It is soft. The band is fine.

reviews.title: Couldn't get weather or trust blood pressure monitor
reviews.text:
The user manual provides a barcode to pull up the manual on your phone. Hard to see it
on the phone, easier using your computer. Load Youtube and search
for 'niuffit smart watch' and find several videos on the watch.

The clear, colorful manual insert is full of details. Wasn't expecting that. Nice.

Also included a helpful setup guide with GloryFit App bluetooth connections.

Tons of watch faces to choose from (many are youthful choices)

Heart Rate and Steps were fairly correct.

The watch and the Gloryfit App(free) are nice for the price.

CONS:
Inexpensive hard and short charging cable which requires plugging into a USB port!

Mesh band was not easy for me to strap on. I switched to the extra strap. You can always replace the band with another style.

The Weather Icon showed empty and the Gloryfit App's Weather showed error
(all permissions permitted) trying to find my (listed) location.
(My Samsung 4 Watch gets location fine).

Not sure if the problem is the App or the Watch. The error displayed my city location.

*** FOLLOW-UP *** Their support had been in contact with me and the Weather Location had been resolved by uninstalling and reinstalling the app. Their support was very good. Unusual to receive that (I used to be a tech support programmer).
***

All blood pressure measurements from the App, being picked up by the watch were incorrect
when compared to an actual blood pressure machine (tested sedentary and exercise).

To be honest, probably most inexpensive Blood Pressure watches are inacccurate too.

To be safe, I'd suggest turn on ALL permissions if you have problems connecting .

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: Nice smart watch with a lot of great features
reviews.text:
If you cringe at the $$$ price of the brand name smart watches, I think this one by Eurans is a pretty good value. It still have quite a few features that are really nice. It tracks fitness, sleep, heart rate & O2 monitoring like a champ. The step counter is good too, fairly accurate compared to the Fitbit I previously wore. Pairing the watch to the app was a breeze.

I like how there's a good variety of fitness options to choose from, both indoor and outdoor. That's great when like me you like to do different types of exercise through the week to keep from getting bored or losing motivation. I'm very happy with the Fitdock app. No weird translation issues like with other apps I've tried. It's easy to understand, and it works well. It's fun to be able to use a picture from my phone as the background screen on my watch. The silent alarm works well too, it wakes me without waking my spouse, a great feature when couples are on different work schedules.

A couple of features I wish the smart watch had- the ability to monitor blood pressure (though I know it's not really a medically approved method), and a reminder to drink water. But these don't really detract from how nice this watch is, so I didn't remove any stars for it. I think this smart watch would make a great gift for someone who is interested in smart watches, but concerned about paying the high price for the brand name models.

reviews.title: For a $35 watch, it does a pretty good job
reviews.text:
I bought this to replace my Fitbit. There's a lot to like about this watch. The screen is bright and responsive, the app is easy to use, it looks exactly like an Apple Watch, the battery life is incredible, and overall the tracking is fine. I wanted a smart watch to give me a general idea of my activity and sleep levels, and this watch does just that. If you want basic tracking and the appearance of an Apple Watch, this is a great choice.

I do wish there was an auto detect option for activity tracking and a lock option so if you set up manual tracking it doesn't stop the activity before you're done. I also wish there were more available accessories. The lug size is weird, so it's impossible to find a replacement band. I'd really like a stretchy one so I can simply pull it up my arm when I am kickboxing because that activity isn't getting tracked at all.

But overall it does the job, and I'm pleased with my purchase. My fiancé also bought this watch, and he really likes it.

reviews.title: Inaccurate on calories
reviews.text: I went against my nutritionists recommendation to get a smart watch (because it causes EMFs to be emitting through your body constantly). But I was really drawn to the idea of counting my steps, reading my blood pressure & oxygen levels, and seeing my calories burned per run/workout. Well, according to this device, I only burn half the calories the treadmill says I do -- and it calls them by the European "kcals", which though popular is annoying. Knowing the 'kcals' are so off, I don't really trust this watch. For decades I've known that a 30 min walk (3.0) is around 120 cals. Now it's 57. Is the watch more accurate than every treadmill? I mean, how are they measuring? The app developers response was "Thanks for the feedback, we'll keep improving." Even when it says I went 5,000 steps it still says 82 cals while experts tell us a minimum of 100 cals are burned in 5k steps. I also would love to believe the sleep readout but I'm pretty sure it's off because it says I get a lot of deep sleep and since I have not had a good night's sleep in 15 years, I'm guessing that's not correct; however, I'm not sure what it's reading or how those conclusions are reached. the Snorapp I trust (not related to this device) because it tells me I'm 'epic' around 40% of the night. I was especially hoping to see my blood oxygen reading so I could improve it, but according to the watch, I'm 99% when it's on tightly, and 94% when it's snug but comfortable. 94% is near death so I definitely need to check with a doctor on that one. Sadly, I'll be returning this and perhaps opting for paying more for a more reliable model since there is no alternative app offered with this watch (and I'm not sure if the watch or the app is to blame). Or maybe a pedometer (either way, you have to swing your arms for it to count). It may not matter but I can say the watch is nice looking, although large (easy to read), easy set up, nice face choices, delivers notifications (put on dnd for sleep mode), easy synching, long battery life. The pastel mint green is very pleasant. I will also say that I felt some discomfort at first when I put it on. I happen to know the sensation was from the EMFs; other people have described it as discomfort or pain without explanation. One person had blisters and thought it was from the metal; however, there's no metal touching the skin except the siding, which appears to be copper. Copper does, in fact, conduct energy at a higher frequency than most metals. So there might be a double-edged sword here for people sensitive to EMFs.
reviews.author: AHVDT6EBYXAVSS6F4UPXC4QUNCNA

reviews.title: Watch is good, not great with mybphone
reviews.text:
The watch is great. Synched quickly with the app and I like the number of different face options offered. The battery life is also strong and it charges quickly.
Things I didn't like. If you touch the face it quickly unlocks and scrolls through the menus. So when I cross my arms I can mess up the watch, it needs a better lock. The app didn't synch all options to my watch/phone. So, I couldn't track sleep, heartrate and other things.
Maybe if I have a better phone?
reviews.author: AFIMJQKSLYNV3VR5FGCXU2ZQXGQQ

reviews.title: Awesome Smart Watch
reviews.text:
For the money, this is an awesome smart watch that has some of the same popular features as that “fruit” brand.
The watch was packaged securely in a box and included a User Manual and a “How to Connect with the smart watch” brochure.

My watch was received with 46% battery life and did not need to be charged immediately, but I noticed that the charge cord is magnetic and attaches easily to the back of the watch.

I read the instructions and powered on the watch. I downloaded the app and proceeded to pair, referred to as “bind” in the manual. The smart watch paired effortlessly with my iPhone 13. Other than pairing or unpairing, the user manual doesn’t cover much else, but I found the watch use to be intuitive. I do think a more detailed user manual would be helpful to explain navigating and swipe direction of the screens.

The watch has many functions and I was pleasantly surprised that the watch provided a “Siri connection” and performed flawlessly. I compared the blood pressure and oxygen sensors to my individual monitors to check accuracy. Surprisingly, the watch is pretty accurate.

As I mentioned earlier, I started using the watch with 46% battery life and wore the watch for approximately 25 hours before I received a low battery alert.

This is a great buy and so much so, that within hours of using my watch, I ordered another one for my husband.
reviews.author: AGOEISYA6MVC5ROVWYRI4KTVNXOA

reviews.title: Won’t track anything
reviews.text: Received this watch and it connected to my phone via Bluetooth immediately. Unfortunately, it won’t track most things it’s supposed to! My steps, BP, temperature won’t track at all. If I put the watch skin tight it will read my heart rate and oxygen, but  if I put the band on comfortably, it won’t read them. The screen only lights up for 4 seconds, it’s not enough time to look at what I’m trying to do. There’s no way to adjust how long it lights up for, so I have to keep tapping the screen to keep it lit up while I try to see if it’s measuring anything. The instructions say it’s supposed to light up automatically if you move your arm up, but it will not. You have to press the side button every time to have the screen light up. The temperature is stuck at 3°C, even though it’s currently 40° where I’m at.
reviews.author: AEEEQYWNV354ZPKS6TH7JT5VVH6Q

reviews.title: Pretty good watch
reviews.text: This is my first "smart watch" so I'm still getting used to it.  The biggest user issue I have is that it isn't simply ON when I look at it.  It usually turns on if I move my arm properly, and I can switch it ON using my other hand.  But, when in busy or messy tasks, that's not the best thing.  The sleep tracker may or may not be really accurate....the step tracker is running at about 50% of my Fitbit pocket unit.  I'm thinking that the watch only really counts steps if my arm is free and swinging.  So, walking a half mile, pushing a shopping cart sounds as zero steps.  Pulse and blood oxygen seem to be close anyway.  But, the band is comfortable, and doesn't have that slimy silicone feel that some do.  Big numbers on the face and easy to read, and lots of faces available or put your own image up from your phone library.  Overall good value in my humble opinion.  Oh.....battery life is amazing.  Just charged it today because it was convenient.  But, it had been running on last week's charge, eight days, and was only down one half of the battery indicator.
reviews.author: AF3BUQEGHUBWBOORHN44HPMQRUVQ

reviews.title: Don’t trust for your health
reviews.text: Used while on the treadmill after installing app and connecting to phone. Took 10 minutes before a heart rate would show up. Then it would keep the same reading even when I went from a 3mph walking pace to 7mph jogging speed. Then eventually it would show a higher heart rate . If you want this to ensure you don’t over exert yourself then do not buy it. You could have a heart attack and it wouldn’t tell you til it’s too late. I knew I shouldn’t have tried a cheap model

reviews.title: Price was good but...
reviews.text:
My daughter talked me into getting this because it's so cheap. I've been wanting a watch like this so I can keep track of how I sleep and also for steps. I'm trying to lose weight and sleep is a big factor! I don't get much sleep and that hurts my weight loss. I'm trying to get more. However, sleep part is SUPER INCORRECT as well as the steps!!! I walked on my treadmill and it didn't count ANY steps till I started swinging my arm as I have noticed if I'm sitting and I move my arm it counts steps. I'll use it till I find a better one that’s more accurate!! It does a great job with syncing on app on phone and the battery life is amazing.
Update Mar 2nd. 2022
The reason I changed this to 1 star is because I REALLY NEED something to calculate my sleep for weight loss!! This is the 2nd time it showed me sleeping when I've not been able to go to sleep! 1 hour and 55 min. Then 2/24/22 it showed me sleeping 6 hours 17 minutes when I didn't sleep. March 1st it showed me sleeping 2 hours 58 minutes when I was sick and slept the night of Feb 28th and all day 1st of Mar. I'm going to ask around what I need to get more accurate sleep and steps mode. This is cheap and I definitely got nothing for what I paid for. 🙄

reviews.title: lovely Smart Watch for I Phone and Android Users
reviews.text:
I love this watch! This watch is pretty comfortable since the face of the watch doesn't weigh much , as well as the watch band is light but solid. I love that it can fit different sized wrists, especially because I have a kids like wrist and it's hard to find watches that actually fit without being too long or bulky. I originally purchased this watch since the last watch that I had broke and was very similar to this one. This one has a larger and more sturdy watch clip clasp that holds the wrist band into place which is great. The battery life on it is pretty good even when using Bluetooth to your phone. It didn't lower my phone battery too bad which is good.

Most days I don't connect my watch to my phone since I don't find it professional to have it connected while at work. The main functions of the watch that I have used are The step counter, alarm, timer, Heart rate monitor, Sp02 , and the call alert function <( phone has to be connected to app). I have not used the exercise function or the sleep function. All the functions that I have used have worked correctly.

The watch came with a small book of directions, which was helpful. On the box that the watch came in is a barcode that you can scan and it will take you to the app that you need to download to use the sleep function, alert for incoming calls, notifications for different social apps well, health care, as well as being able to customize the watch face to whatever picture you want.

My favorite features of this watch are the step counter, being able to customize my watch face , as well as being able to adjust the watch to a comfortable fit. Make sure not to wear the watch too tight .Lave at least a finger thickness away from the bone by the wrist for it can get an accurate reading as well for it won't break from a lot of wrist movement.
reviews.author: AFXZQPWGFZHQ2F7LAS4TGB2NPJVA

reviews.title: This watch literally makes me mad. It should work better and be easy to use.
reviews.text:
I'm so disappointed. The step counter is double the correct number, most likely but I have no idea. I just know it's way off. The sleep monitor was good for two days. On the third day I had my first good night's sleep and the app reported 'no data'. I now can't find the sleep app to see why it is not working, but it's just not working and there is no manual to explain anything for this watch. The instructions are horrible and the app may have use or value but without an actual way to read proper instruction with a layout of what the watch can do and how the app is layed out, forget it. It's just a waist of time and this is making me so angry.
No one wants to waist time and no one wants their time disrespected to this degree.

The youtube videos, meh. I think there was one but I'm not making one. The company should make the support materials.

Get a real watch, from a known maker like fitbit or apple, and make sure they have instructions. Go to the actual store or something.

The heart rate, 115!!! The O2 doesn't make sense. 99 or 100 and then it slowly decreases and the data is never recorded. The sync works but who cares if there is no data being recorded. I never even got to the exercise. The steps today 23000! Ha! No way! I think the clock works, but all of the faces should show the seconds.

The watch is huge and has a very high profile, thick and bulky face. It should be thinner so it doesn't get hung up on clothes.

The watch is attractive, it is the watch shown, but dang! It should work and the instructions should be great, no bad or just okay.
3 stars.
reviews.author: AH5PGJYVXBFVRXAFSTA7AEH4EXRQ

reviews.title: UPDATE
reviews.text: Here’s my new review.  Love the watch. Love that I can make and take calls.  I’ve worn this watch every day since I received it.  The calls volume is just fine.  The only downfall is the battery life.  I do have to charge daily, even on days that I don’t use it for calls at all.  BUT,  it’s easy to do.  I put it on the charger at bedtime, watch a little tv and poof, it’s charged and back on my wrist before going to sleep.  It charges fast.  Great watch for the price.  What I’ve noticed with reviews is, a lot of bad reviews are from people who just don’t know how to pair their watch accurately with their phone.  When it’s not working for calls, go to Bluetooth on your phone, make sure it’s on.  You should see Y20 Pro AND Y20_Pro Phone in the list. Both need to say “connected” before the calling feature will work on your watch.

It does very well with weather, music control, steps and measurements. The app is so-so. I have to option to continually measure my heart rate. The watch measures it constantly but the app never graphs it out. I've had a 0 heart rate (according to the app) since last night. The sleep measurements aren't accurate but I'll just take it off at night from now on. But for the price it works like i expect it to without having to pay 10x more. The da fit app could use a lot of work though.

reviews.title: Worked for almost 3 weeks.
reviews.text:
I have had a Pebble smart watch for many years, and really liked it, but the time became a few minutes off, so I factory reset it only to find out my iPhone no longer supported the app. After much trying, I gave up and purchased this watch.

I like the watch okay, it looks nice, there was a learning curve, but I was figuring it out.

Two big drawbacks were that the watch face would randomly change on me, and the other was that I called my daughter purely by accident several times while in the shower. She was worried that I was hurt, because I didn’t respond when she was trying to talk to me. I didn’t mean to call her, and did not hear her talking.

I did use the answer call part once, and that worked fine when it was purposeful.

The battery life was shorter than expected, lasting 2-3 days.

The big bummer was this morning. The battery was low, so I plugged it in last night, and this morning there was no charge and the watch would not connect to my phone.

I am sad, and will return the watch, and search for a better affordable option.

I would warn perspective buyers away from this watch.

reviews.title: Just got it as a basic "electric" watch
reviews.text:
So far, after having this watch for 30 minutes, this is what I have noticed:
1. It keeps telling me my heartbeat, even though I am not wearing it. I wasn't expecting to use this function from a $25 watch as anything accurate, but still... come on. Just making stuff up???
EDIT: The watch says it reads "green and red light" from your skin to get your heartrate, so if you let in other light it gets tricked. Regardless, don't count on a $25 watch to take any accurate health readings people.
EDIT 2: I got a second watch from them replacing the first free of charge. The second one has a much better heart rate monitor, and it is not making stuff up like the first.

2. I charged it from 48% how it arrived, to 76% in a matter of minutes, but then after wearing it, it was dropping about 5% every few minutes. Not sure if it has any clue of it's actual battery life.
EDIT: It keep stopping charging at 96%, then randomly vibrates, and shows it being at 96% when it vibrates. I don't know if that means it thinks it's charged and uses 4% of the battery to let me know that?
EDIT 2: The second watch I got as a replacement is much better for charging. Maybe the first one was just wonky.

3. It tries to collect a ton of your data, including location, when it is not necessary. You HAVE to turn this on in the beginning to get it to pair with your phone, but then after that you can turn it off.

Has a cool few "faces" on the screen, and for $25 I am just using it as a basic watch.

reviews.title: Good Watch for the price
reviews.text: I bought this watch for the fact that I could actually make and receive calls.  I had no desire to spend $400 or more.  This is a nice watch and one that offers several features I do like, such as heart rate, blood pressure, blood oxygen levels, and it tracks steps and has other workout features.  It connected easily to my iPhone.  One issue I had was in using the phone feature because the directions that come with the phone are very minimal.  The small manual details the features but other than saying connect through the app it gives little help.  I ended up going to google and watching some videos.  Once I figured out how to actually make a call, I could barely hear through the watch with the volume turned up high as it would go.  I also could not be heard by the person I called.  Now, all that being said, in all fairness, this is an excellent watch for under $40, so perhaps my expectations were a bit unrealistic given the extremely reasonable cost of the watch.

I will say that the heart rate is garbage if you're leaving it to measure all day on its own, my random readings were always double what my heart rate actually was. I have no way of verifying the accuracy of the O2 stats or the blood pressure, but they seemed to be in normal range for me.

Notifications are good and easy to read and call/music control works well.

Callers said that while I sounded a little far away, I was clear and easy to understand. Calls sounded clear and there was no additional noise.

Music sound is a bit off, but nothing that is intolerable.

All In all, I'd say that if you're not looking for Google/Samsung/apple specific features, this is a great choice.

The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. reviews.title: Nice resolution
reviews.text:
I bought this watch because of the high-resolution display. And that's what I got, no more, no less.

Upon first impression, I can tell the display has crisp graphics. The touch screen is responsive. The processor is fast. Overall, the phone itself is nice.

The app, on the other hand, is awful. It is intuitive, but it loses Bluetooth connectivity without reason. This is the only app that I've seen that will move forward and backward during a dial sync. When I switch to certain dials, the progress bar will reach 61%, then count back down to 60%, then 59%, then start going back up. It's nice to see honesty in an app, but it's just strange.

I didn't test notifications from other apps, but no SMS text messages came through. The setup is simple and easy with SMS notifications settings on its own section. But once set up, no messages came through.

Battery life? What battery life? After a fully charge, this watch drained to 90% in the 15 minutes I used to overlook the features and set up the app. This from a 330mAh battery. At this rate, the battery will be depleted in less than two hours.

reviews.title: Good value for a Smart Watch
reviews.text:
As far as I can tell, this is a really good value for what you get. It's a strong 4 star but lacking important details. The band is also on the weaker side.

This Smart Watch CANNOT properly function without first charging and using the APP for pairing. It does not pair directly to your Bluetooth but through the APP!!! I was able to pair/bind this using an Android phone with not issues. You will need to carefully follow screen prompts. After pairing with APP, the watch will automatically set time/date and language. Watch is ready to wear!!!

Here are the issues. There are no real instructions/directions, basically you will need to use common sense when trying to configure watch to notify you of incoming messages, SMS and other things like FB, IG, etc. The directions are very lacking to say the least. Paper instructions are much too vague with only a few hints, etc. You cannot use all options without first using the settings in the APP. The watch will most likely "over count" your steps but making sure you have correct weight and height in settings may help.

They should have, at a minimum, something online be it a video or website, explaining in greater DETAIL all the possible functions of the watch. I can understand reviews that state frustration with pairing, functions etc and it's probably due to lack of clear and detailed instructions.

All in all, would buy again. I really don't need the notifications but steps and heartbeat mostly.

reviews.title: battery life is great, backlight goes on randomly the night sometimes waking me or my wife up
reviews.text:
'Wrist sense' feature doesn't work well, it kept lighting up the screen at night when I would roll over... or sometime just a minor arm movement. The backlight is very bright, so I had to turn off that feature.

Sleep tracking seems okay, not sure how accurate that is. The app is so so. For heart rate all it tracks is 'resting' which seems a bit silly.

'Activity' feature doesn't seem very useful, other than displaying end of activity information. Can't find any of that in the app.

Battery life for my usage has been excellent, at 24 days battery life was down to 10%.

** UPDATE ** The backlight comes on randomly. It will wake me and/or my wife up. Pressing the button sometimes doesn't turn it off. Seems pointless tracking sleep and then waking the user up with random bright light. The do not disturb feature doesn't keep it from lighting up...and wrist sense is already off. Reboot did not resolve.

** UPDATE 2 ** Seller reached out and offered to send a new watch. I didn't see the point, however I was willing to try just in case the random backlight issue was hardware related. No change in the functionality.

I did discover that maybe shutting down the app reduced or eliminated the spurious backlight issue. I can't be sure. Of course shutting down the app disables alerting features which to me seems like a huge issue.

We now have one connected to my wife's phone and so far is hasn't lite up that we've noticed. She often sleeps with her arms under the covers so we may not notice.

+1 star for customer service trying...

reviews.title: Amazing value for entry-level smart watch!
reviews.text:
Context is key - this is a very inexpensive smart watch (~$40). Comparing it to Apple/Google/Samsung/Fitbit is unfair. So let's just talk about what you do get and why it's a good value.

--Useful fitness/health tracker: This watch has great heart rate, Sp02 sensor, exercise detection, GPS integration and more. It's also well water-proofed for open water swimming. Honestly, for fitness tracking alone, it's an exceptional value and should be considered for anyone thinking about getting a Fitbit since most of those don't have lots of bells and whistles either. This watch is less expensive and more capable than those competitors.

--Helpful extras: Weather, music control, text/call notifications, and Alexa integration - these make the device a "smart watch" and give you some added convenience around the house. Experiment, and you'll find yourself using it - especially if you're in a room without another smart speaker and want to control household devices easily.

--Comfortable and attractive: The first thing I noticed out of the box was how comfortable the included silicone band is. It comes in two sizes, so it can definitely fit pretty much any wrist. I think it uses standard wristwatch band connection points as well if you'd like to change it out. You can choose from a variety of watch faces - I showed the one I prefer in pictures because it has most of the relevant status indicators I like. From the outside, it looks and feels like a high-end smart watch. Great build quality.

Cons:
No review would be complete without discussing cons. This watch has a few - but try and keep in mind the price point. That's why I didn't take any stars away.
--Cannot reply to texts - you can read them, but no ability to reply
--Cannot see what music is playing - you just get basic controls (volume, play/pause, next/prev track)
--No wireless charging - don't lose the included cable
--Touch interface is slightly laggy. Not unusable, just not buttery smooth. Probably a trade-off for improved battery life.

Bottom line? This is a capable smart watch. I'm amazed it can be sold for such a low price. If you're looking for a fitness/health tracker, I'd say this is for you. If you'd like to dabble in smart watches to see if you like having the extra features on top, this makes for a great entry point. If you find you don't care for using a smart watch, would you rather be out $40 or $300? Easy question in my book.

reviews.title: So far the watch is great for price!!!
reviews.text:
So far I do like this watch mainly for what i wanted it for in ability to make And receive phone calls and see messages incoming while I'm at work, many features don't work without app which is understandable, and there's no volume control on watch for music e.t.c, the sleep tracker didn't work right last night it missed the last couple hours of sleep but i do get confirmation i did my suspicions of my overall sleep quality, I'll make a list below of the abilities watch has and my review of those! For the price im very happy so far I'll update once I've used it more and if anything goes wrong!! Following list of features:
Display, only stays lit up 3 seconds with no way to change time for display, this i didn't prefer.
Display turns on anytime i move my wrist which can be a little annoying in the dark when trying to get my kids to sleep lol!
Watch face themes, has many to choose from but none that have my preferred visuals except 1 that has battery level heart rate and time.
Has 4 direction swipe from home screen to see messaging, make calls e.tc
FANTASTIC PHONE CALL CLARITY ON BOTH ENDS!! Dailed phone number made a call last night and it was crystal clear and loud enough to hear well! Impressed with that for sure!! Perfect when im driving or at work or in emergency if phone is near but i can't get to it, like if i fell e.t.c!!! I think this feature alone makes the watch worth the price And more!!!!!
Messaging: i can see all my incoming and recived recent messages of text and Facebook messenger work little icon tells me which is which and who sent it, the only 1 thing i wish this watch had is ability to reply even with a generic reply like ok, or im busy, brb e.tc anything would be cool but i think the calling feature is my main like!
Has music play,pause, skip but again no volume control except on my phone, unless ive overlooked this feature somehow, again clarity is good for running or just enjoying music at work!
Has ability to monitor and take blood pressure, pulse, oxygen saturation which seem to be working ok, sometimes the readings are a little off when taken back to back but general idea is all im after, i tried it on my girls so i know it's working!
Has sleep tracking which i didn't feel works correctly, it didn't count the last few hours i slept but did show me my rem(rapid eye movement) sleep, light sleep, and deep sleep And waking times, gave me basic idea which I wouldn't use as definitive sleep records just idea of my sleep patterns at nite.
App has female menstrual cycle feature with tracking in app which will alert me when ovulation starts and ends, menstruation should begin and end and other useful things in app! Sends alerts to watch!
Has feature to remind to drink water and set times for alerts, this is huge deal for me and another reason i like this watch!
Can set alarms to wake up e.tc but only in app not on watch itself, but can turn alarms off and on on watch.
Has live weather updates and on watch gives me 7 day forecast!!
Has step counter.
Has camera shutter button on watch to operate my phone camera, i like it because it has a 3 second countdown!
Watch has brightness level selection.
Has stop watch.
Has timer in 1,3,5,10,15,20,30 ,60 minute intervals.
Has walking running ,cycling, rope skipping,badminton, football, basketball sports modes which tells you time mileage calorie burn etc, haven't used don't know accuracy yet.
Has movie theater mode which is basically like airplane mode , silences features haven't used yet though.
Overall I really like this watch the cost under 50 bucks and for that price this watch is a bei good deal as long as it is durable and doesn't burn out quickly!!
UPDATE 1: So far i really love my watch however a few things that aren't working right and some great, firstly the sleep tracker doesn't work right at all, so oddly it doesn't allow user to track day sleeping, 1 day a week i day sleep to catch up on my crazy work week, I slept from about 10am to 4pm and it didn't track one second! Bummer there! Then last night again i fell asleep early as usual around 7pm and it only seems to start tracking sleep after 11pm but always seems to end correctly with wake up time, I'm disappointed in this feature but it's not a deal breaker since the features i actually care about work fantastically, for instance the blood pressure, i happen to work in dental clinic and have access to actual blood pressure cuff and the reading from my watch was 116/76 whereas the blood pressure cuff was 118/ 78 pretty darn close so I'll call that accurate, BUT I have tattoos on my forearm where my watch sits and it won't read thru the tat ink well if at all, so keep this in mind! Also I see ppl complaining about blood pressure numbers being way off from doctors bp readings and here's why, you're supposed to take your bp on your left arm, your right arm will have a much different blood pressure especially if you've ever had any injuries to right arm or are overweight! Always take bp on left arm which is the heart side. Other than that pose And oxygen saturation works spot on! All other features are great!! Really really LOVE LOVE THIS WATCH!! will update again later!!

reviews.title: Too many bugs
reviews.text:
I wanted to love this because of the answer/call feature but I ultimately returned it.
The watch continually disconnects from the phone (this is a built in feature to conserve battery) so I will hear my phone ringing and see it on my watch but I am unable to answer it. You have to keep reconnecting it through the Bluetooth on your phone.
I also only received about 70% of my call/text/message notifications.
I don’t know if I got a faulty watch but it also didn’t securely attach to the charger. I have another fitness tracker and it strongly magnetically connects but this one would spin off it not placed just right.

Also, when I would make a phone call using my phone, it would default to put the sound through my watch and sometimes during a phone call the audio would switch mid conversation and start coming through the watch.

The health features matched my other fitness tracker pretty well though the step count did seem low.

reviews.title: There are TWO ways of rating this... Garbage or almost garbage.
reviews.text:
There are two ways of rating purchases... either an overall evaluation IRREGARDLESS of the price OR... CONSIDERING THE PRICE. Considering I paid $20 for it... not horrible. BUT here are the SERIOUS flaws so far:

-The Blood Pressure monitor is a COMPLETE JOKE. "Inaccurate" is NOT the word. I tried it on several friends and coworkers. Garbage!
-You are supposed to be able to make a gesture of bringing your wrist up to view the watch and then it is supposed to turn the screen on so that you can read it. ALMOST NEVER! No matter HOW I make the gesture the only thing that works is a PAINFUL twist/shake of my wrist and then it's not guaranteed. I just use the button instead which kind of defeats the purpose.
-BUT THEN, when sleeping.... EVERY DAMNED MOVEMENT I MAKE it turns on... and waking up my wife, because it is like having a flashlight shone in your face!!!!!!!!!!!

So... EVEN THOUGH this was only $20... SAVE YOUR MONEY FOR SOMETHING THAT ACTUALLY WORKS AND WORKS WELL.

reviews.title: Comfortable and reliable
reviews.text: I mainly use this as a pedometer. It’s light weight and comfortable. I like the other features, too, such as stress, oxygen, heart rate levels, etc. I haven’t been able to load the upgrades from the app successfully, but it works fine for its intended use for me.

reviews.title: Great watch at a fraction of the price from all the others!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:3a6af7cc8f82778d9b9ca7c235ec822a]] I've worn fitbits for years and replaced them over and over. This watch was a fraction of the price and does everything my fit bit did plus more-Alexa. The watch fits the same bands as the versa. It holds a longer charge I've been wearing it for 3 days on the original charge. It uses the very fit app which is super easy to pair. Get my notifications from whichever apps I choose on it. The charger doesn't have a cradle it has to be on a flat surface and away from anything so it doesn't get knocked over while charging. Overall love this watch and love the price!

reviews.title: Entry level smart watch
reviews.text:
I've looked at smart watches and balked at the price. It seemed too much money on something that I might not like or use much or for very long. The modest price on this one made me decide to try it out. I am glad that I did because I've been wearing it and using it for more than a month now.

Besides the obvious telling time function I've used the various functions on a regular basis, particularly the step counter and sleep tracker. The only problem I have had is that there really wasn't much in the way of instructions included with this so mostly I have been figuring this out by trial and error.

Overall I am pleased with this watch, and I am using it more than I thought I would. I would have preferred more written instructions with this so I could access the various functions without going through so much trial and error.
reviews.author: AHXWUCTMVBQXDVMDFPA3NO43QF2Q

reviews.title: Cool watch to keep track of your health
reviews.text:
What I like about this watch:
- one full charge lasts several days
- good way to track your BPM, Blood pressure, calories burn, steps taken
- could make phone calls with the watch
- if you take the watch off for whatever reason (like to shower) it doesn't keep taking your BMP or blood pressure so you don't have to worry about your data getting messed up/ inaccurate.
- comfortable silicone band which has a lot of holes to adjust to your size wrist, I have a very small wrist and use the second to last hole
- good size screen, not too large and not too small
- you could set alarms (through the app) also features a stop watch and timer
- receive and check notifications for several apps plus text messages
- has a find phone feature if you misplace your phone
- you could add music to your phone (which I have not done, and not sure if I'm going to do)
- waterproof
- Compatible with my Samsung smartphone, and easy to set up
- Great to have at work, to keep track of time and if I receive any messages/notifications on my phone without having to check my phone

dislike:
- only 4 different dial face designs to choose from
- speaker is towards the bottom so sound could be blocked by your arm depending on your position
- sleep tracker is not very accurate (while I'm laying in bed reading, watching a movie, or on my phone it thinks I am sleeping, and there is no way to edit the hours manually)
- no power button (to turn on or off)
- you cannot name or write notes with the alarms, there are only icons to label
- does not stay connected to my phone in my whole house. If I have my phone on charge in my living room and I'm in my kitchen which is 2 rooms over, I will not get notifications from my phone
- app doesn't not always update, I will have to go to the app and refresh (down swipe) for data to refresh and update
- charger connection is magnetic and a couple times it was bumped while on charge and the charger got knocked off and I didn't know, do it wasnt charging. Plus if the charger breaks for whatever reason I don't have a back up.
reviews.author: AG644AQBRT56BVFDCJMISCNTSRMA

reviews.title: Nice looking smart watch with useful features
reviews.text: My new smart watch is attractive wearing it. I use my watch to measure heart rate, oxygen level and blood pressure. The heart rate and oxygen readings I have gotten are comparable to my tabletop devices that measure the same, but I have found the blood pressure reading to be consistently low compared with my arm cuff reader. The health features I have used for walking “exercise” that could the steps, distance and calories works very well, and also heart rate is measured during my walking exercise, which I find appealing and helpful. The watch has a large number of watch faces to choose from, and the watch face changes immediately when you select a new watch face in the app. The app called Da Fir was easy for me to download and install and easy to use. I can read my texts on my watch, but not reply to the texts, which was disappointing to me. The watch did not allow me to receive or make calls on the watch, which is something other potential customers should consider before getting this particular watch. Overall this is a nice looking watch with a decent range of features and I recommend it.
reviews.author: AEC22OPKQJXETMVXGAUVJ3CWJZRQ

reviews.title: I love this watch!
reviews.text:
I've been looking for an affordable watch that clearly tells the time while enabling me to track my steps, oxygen level, and heart rate. This turned out to be exactly what I was looking for, and more! I hadn't realized how much I'd appreciate also being able to see the weather forecast, change the image and data on the watch face, set an alarm, and other features. I've set it to push through text messages but not phone or social media alerts (for now, anyway). The face illuminates by simply lifting your arm, rather than having to click the "on" button.

I ordered a larger-size watch and at first was afraid that it would be too heavy and look weird on my wrist, but it feels fine. I like having the larger face, which makes it easier both to see the data and select icons for the weather, etc.

The one problem I had is that the QR code in the instruction manual took me to a website that wasn't operational. I sent a message through Amazon to the manufacturer and received a message back almost immediately, directing me to download the Da Fit app. I was impressed at that fast service (although still a little frustrated that the QR code didn't work). Once I had the app, I was easily able to pair the watch with my phone, as well as to adjust settings, such as setting the weather data to Fahrenheit rather than Celsius.

I highly recommend this for its excellent price and features. I'm using it myself, but it would also be a nice gift.
reviews.author: AGGA6AKN63OEU2OJL33UIRHISZGA

reviews.title: Nice looking watch, good health features
reviews.text: My new smart watch has a gold flat gold color case, glossy gold color crown, which I find appealing. The watch has a watch band that is pinkish color which I did not care for. The watch has a solid set of features I like including making and receiving calls. The speaker volume for calls on the watch I find to be decently loud, and meets my needs and the mic works well. The watch allows me to receive and read texts but not respond to the texts, I can only read and delete them from the watch. The watch tracks my steps, calories and heart rate well while I exercise. I also use the blood oxygen meter on the watch which I find compares well to my home fingertip tester. One unfortunate health item missing from this watch is blood pressure, which was alittle disappointing. The watch syncs with an app called GloryFit, which was easy to setup and works relatively well for me. From the app there is a very good selection of watch faces to choose from, which I found appealing. The screen on the watch is responsive to my touch. One thing I find unique on the watch is it has 2 games, including the “bird game” which I have not had on my other smart watches.  Overall I like the watch and recommend it.

reviews.title: Great buy
reviews.text: This watch is great. It’s a great alternative to a Apple Watch. For the price you can’t beat it. You can answer and make calls, tracks sleep steps calories exercise. You can view your texts but can’t text back but what I do is use the ai voice to talk to siri to send a text back. I love that I can leave my phone in my purse and still do everything. There are a ton of other things you can do but I haven’t spent the time to sit down and go thru everything. Set up is super easy and the charge has lasted me a couple days at a time.
reviews.author: AELBYQXDI3BQW6537XZUXIVP6T2Q

reviews.title: Poor Measurements - Support Cannot Help
reviews.text:
After charging for 30 minutes as instructed, I paired the smart watch with the app. This was easy, and the one reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1. The other reason for 2 stars, the interface was easier to use than I expected.

But if the measurements are not accurate, ease of use doesn't matter.

I bought this smart watch to track my heart rate while exercising—mainly walking. After recent heart procedures, my doctor wants me to keep my pulse below a certain rate. Each time I measure, the pulse differs substantially from the pulse oximeter that always agrees with my doctor's office pulse oximeter, and heart monitor. It goes as high as 90 when the PO says 52. I took multiple measurements, including taking my own pulse. The closest measurements were 60 when the actual rate was 51 or 52.

Support is through a chat on the app. I did not wait long for support, however they did not try to help me. They insisted that the heart rate changes. That I had to let it sync with the app. Not once did they address the possibility that the smart watch might be reading the rate wrong. Told me to try again after I had already tried multiple times.

Focusing on anything but the issue I am reporting does not help me. Even suggesting I exercise and measure my heart rate. Telling me the human heart rate is constantly changing. Literally, answers like they were talking to a small child.

I had high hopes for this device. It disappointed on two levels - Heart Rate, Support. I cannot recommend. Look for a different smart watch if you need accurate heart rate measurements.
reviews.author: AGK4MCK2TFEDGFESK7IY3DAFNCRQ

reviews.title: NOT AS DESCRIBED AT ALL
reviews.text: I carefully read all reviews before purchasing this smart watch but was so disappointed. It would not hold preferences & kept defaulting to kg & cm. Would not toggle from one thing to another on app. Had to go all the way out of app & go back in before it would toggle from steps to profile to anything else. Sometimes I'd get my cell to ring on my watch, never could answer it or text back from the watch. I'd rather pay the extra & get an apple watch that actually works. I can't tell you how many times I set the preferences & saved them only to find it defaulted right back the next day.  One of the sorriest watches I've ever owned & I kept it 2 weeks before returning it really trying to make it work.

reviews.title: Great features for value
reviews.text: Unlike another watch I purchased (and returned), this one's battery actually lasts several days as described. Love the charging case it comes with, unlike some watches where they only have a magnetic cable that never stays in place as soon as it is bumped or moved which was very frustrating. The case makes sure the cable stays in place. Only regret is the lack of face choices; This is common with most watches, with only a few choices where only a couple that I like, out of only 4 to chose from. This can be easily fixed but somehow they prefer to keep you limited (in my case one I really like and one is ok, so 2 out of 4 are usable faces. It would be nice if the knobs were also used for more features by turning them, but I guess you get what you pay for. For the price, it is a very decent watch and hopefully in the future, you can upload more face choices which should not be hard to do at all. That alone would make me give it 5 stars...

reviews.title: Great activity tracker with AMAZING Battery Life.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:e0fda475d57018948d4bdf5116c091bf]] I had a different version activity watch that was way more expensive to match my phone. For the cost this watch does just about everything I need it to for way less money.

A few things I love about the watch...the Battery Life. OMG the battery life! It lasted me a full week with wearing it to bed most nights, through multiple exercise walks and a few work outs, and hundreds of notifications from my messaging apps and phone calls. It's easy to clear the notifications I receive on the watch. I love that it tracks my sleep, heart rate, and stress levels easily. I also tested out in my pool for swimming and it was waterproof without having to shake the watch or turn on any special modes to keep it watertight.

I haven't figured out of it auto detects a work out but that could be because I obviously don't work out that much.

I tested it against my health app on my phone and they were pretty accurate against each other info steps and mileage. I can't figure out how to get the watch and the app to register my GPS coordinates to see the map of my walks but I could be missing the settings.

The app that gives the data is easy to navigate and update any settings. Seems very similar to other track laps I've used.
A few things I'm not liking about this watch...
I feel like the band and the watch itself that comes with the watch is a little too big for my wrist. And I have a bigger wrist for a female at my size. The watch face is bigger than some of my friends apple watches and taller than my old Samsung gear fit watch. I can SEE notifications on the watch but I can't respond or give a quick response to any messages. For Whatsapp or Facebook Messenger I wouldn't expect that but for SMS messages I would like to be able to give a quick text back either by speaking a response or by selecting from some quick options.

Those are really my only 2 complaints. I like this watch and will continue to use!
reviews.author: AG7YXCJCP4XJ2I2GBJKUUU3HEONQ

reviews.title: outstanding value for the money
reviews.text: This is an outstanding value for the money.  The features and apps available on this phone are numerous and varied, from pulse to step counter to O2 level checker to sleep monitor to stopwatch, alarms, notifications...too many to name, really.  I bought it primarily to be able to see text messages and phone calls by just looking at the watch without having to take my phone out of my pocket, and it does that with great success.  I will note that I did have to set the brightness level all the way up in order to still potentially see it in direct bright sunlight.  The magnetic charger lead attaches easily to the back of the watch, making it a snap to charge. I also love the ability to download different watch faces...on a personal note I hope they will add even more over time. Also a very nice feature is a link directly in the app to a text support line with customer service who can help answer questions about product usage - that's really great customer service!

reviews.title: The best $40 I have ever spent!!! It is easy to use and the step counter is accurate.
reviews.text:
This thing has a boatload of features!

I wanted to get a watch that would accurately count my steps and track my mileage. I looked at Apple watches, but they were SOOO expensive! After reading the reviews of different brands, I found this one and ordered it. It came in exactly one week ago and I am still surprised by how many features are on this watch:

*It provides accurate dates, times, steps, and miles. I tested that watch's accuracy by walking without moving my arms and it still counted each of my steps!! It is very accurate! When I got the watch it was set in 24 hour time and the metric system. It is easily changed on the app on your phone.
*It also lets you see, but not respond to, your most recent messages/emails. It also has various notification options that include emails, phone calls, and other apps.
*A sleep analysis.
*The weather in your current location.
*A health section that measures your blood pressure, heart rate, and oxygen levels.
*A training section that keeps track of how long you do a certain activity, how many steps you got, how many miles you went, and your heart rate. Some of the training selections are walking, running, cycling, and skipping.
*It also has a music "app" that allows you to pause, play, or fast forward a song on your phone's playlist.
*You can adjust the brightness and there is a super-bright flashlight option.
*There is also a way to access your phone's camera and press the button on the watch and it will take the picture on your phone.
*It features a stopwatch.

One review said that the watch turns on during sleep. On the homepage of the watch if you swipe down, it gives you shortcuts to some of the important functions of the watch, one of these is to lock the screen. I have used this at night and my watch does not turn on. To disable it in the morning, simply push the button on the watch. Easy peasy!

Literally the only negative I could find so far is that you cannot customize the training section of the smartwatch with other sports besides the 7 listed.

The charge lasts for about 4-5 days. I have an iPhone 6 and it is compatible with it. I completely recommend this watch to ANYONE!!!! I could not possibly be more satisfied with this purchase.

reviews.title: Poor battery life. Wonky function. Really wanted to like it, but just no.
reviews.text:
I really wanted this watch to work out. I tried my best, but no. Returning it.

The issues:

1) Battery life terrible. It would last about 2 days if I basically didn't use it. If I used it much, it was basically dead at bedtime each day. Phone calls were especially draining. It does answer/make calls, but if you use the phone function much it just kills the battery.

2) Watch's OS is unstable. Frequently I'd get a phone call and the watch would 'ring' audibly, but the 'answer' menu wouldn't come up and no way to get to it. I had to get my phone out to answer, which really defeats the purpose. Restarting the watch would fix it for a bit, but it would shortly recur. A watch I have to restart multiple times a day to work right is pointless.

3) App funky. I'd frequently go into the app on my phone and various functions would say "pair watch"... Except the watch WAS paired and bluetooth connected.

4) Watch package included a $30 incentive to give a 5-star review. I -really- dislike such tactics. That's not why I'm returning it, but wanted to make note of it for others to decide how seriously they take quick 5-star reviews.
reviews.author: AHLAC42W2G363EWPOBZUO72WXBAA

reviews.title: Love it.
reviews.text:
This watch is maybe a little TOO responsive to “raise to wake” sometimes, while other times (rarely) doesn’t wake when you raise your wrist to check the time. In the latter case, you can just press a button to turn on the display. In my opinion, having an option to wake when you tap the screen instead of raising your arm might be a better fit.

It measures your heart rate constantly during “sport” mode, and takes a reading at regular intervals the rest of the time. You can change the frequency in the settings, starting at 5 minutes. It can also measure your blood oxygen level manually, but doesn’t have an automatic reading setting. In my opinion, having it able to be set to automatic, even if only two or three times daily, would be even better.

You can connect the watch data to Google Fit through the TouchElex app, and then set Google Fit as the pedometer in Noom, in order to use the watch with Noom. It would be easier if you could just connect the watch or app directly to Noom instead of going through Google Fit, but you can’t expect every app to be able to connect to every other app, so having a third-party app every relevant app is connected to and being able to share the data that way is pretty awesome.

You can’t talk to people or listen to music on the watch, but I don’t know why you’d want to. You can see messages and even emails if you want to, or turn it off if you don’t. You can choose your own watch face based on your own preferences, or even use an actual photo as the background. Can track sleep cycle. Probably can do more things.

This is written the day after I received the watch. Battery life is looking good, right on track for about seven days before it reaches zero. I’ve recorded my sp02 at least three times today, and will likely record it again later, so I may be eating up the battery life a bit faster than normal use. I also changed the timeout setting to 10 seconds instead of 5.

First fitness watch I’ve actually liked, and it’s affordable. Way to go, developers.

reviews.title: A lot is given for the Price!
reviews.text: I've been doing research on amazon.com for a few months now, on a smartwatch that reads my body temp, blood pressure, sleeping, heart, and more.  I also was looking for something that I could connect to my ipad which is designated on my apartment wifi and really the ipad doesn't leave the apartment.  I did NOT want to connect it to my phone for reasons I don't feel like explaining, nevertheless the watch connected to my ipad with no problem, the app was available for download and it synced perfectly. This watch was just the watch I was looking for.  I couldn't even find an expansive name brand watch that would cover blood pressure and body temperature. Now that the world has been through Covid-19 I want to know more about my body at the current moment and this watch does just that. The operating system isn't designed like the other name brand watches that i came across but i grew to adjust to the simplicity of the interface of the software in the watch... the app also is more so a simple app.. if i could change one thing with the watch it would be having the app and the phone educate you more on what the reading is about and what i should do about it. I would spice up the software more too, but the hardware ; the watch is perfect so far the battery seems to be fine and doesn't die right away... that too is also a factor .. there was so much i was factoring in when buying my watch. Hey i paid 45.00 american dollars and had no problem using american measurements .. for 45 dollars it does exactly what I want it to do .. and it's not bad looking on me either ! so yes five stars... some people might not like the software on the watch and the app there could be more work done with that but i am dealing with what is given to me. Cheers a christmas gift to myself lol Happy Holidays from the Northeast America ! :)

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: I was attracted by battery life and screen options, but was ultimately disappointed.
reviews.text: Working with the app is neither easy nor hard. If you can update MS Office, you can probably handle the app interface. I was confused. I thought that I could customize the screens, but in fact, as far as I can tell, I could only choose among the screens they offer. As to battery life, I didn't have it long enough to know. Finally, as happens with many watches that are activated by you turning your wrist (that is, the screen is off until then), it was a little iffy (maybe cooperating 80% of the time), but that along with the annoying lag turned me off. Oh well, back to my conventional analog watches. But I really liked being able to see the incoming texts and emails without having to pull out my phone. Maybe I'll try the next generation.

The battery life immediately starts to hold less charge, at this point even using the power saving feature and everything set to low power I can only get barely 24 hours out of a charge. The screen isn't too responsive, but does its job however the screen will scratch or chip if you gently tap it against anything. I'd only suggest this to someone as a temporary use product. For the money I didn't expect the greatest quality, but definitely expected better than this. The app seems like it's fine, but I believe that's all done by a 3rd party since there are dozens of different brands using exact same thing.

reviews.title: UPDATE --DON'T BUY THIS WATCH!!
reviews.text: *UPDATE 2/22/2023*- The seller of this watch reached out to me as soon as they found out I was burned by the watch.  They are going to put my injury into consideration since there are people with sensitive skin like me out there.  They took care of this professionally and even have compensated me for the injury.  This is why I have raised my rating from 1 to 3 stars.*  I would give safety of this watch negative 10 stars if I could.  I have ended up with a really bad electric burns from the back of this watch.  The metal is clearly not up to standards required to keep human skin from being burned after hours of wear.  I bought this because of the great functions, but I am super angry because of being burned like this.  It is absolutely ridiculous and could be prevented by using watch grade metals that are meant to protect delicate human wrist skin.  I returned this and demands a full return.  (Wish I could demand compensation for the creams I am now having to use to treat this burn).

reviews.text:
This is a fun watch. I cannot say all the health measurements are accurate as they are not. The blood pressure readings are way high. I check it at same time with my unit that gives reliable results. If my pressure is 112/68 the watch says 153/89. It does this everything. The oxygen saturation reading are accurate. The heart rate is accurate most of the time. The steps seem to be accurate as well. I will say though if I crochet I get steps counted.

It looks like the high end name brand watch. It is easy to read messages on the watch. The watch also rings when my phone rings.

It is worth the price , the App is just okay nothing special. Shows your sleep info well. Nice that you can choose from many watch faces to display. I would gift this watch to a friend.

reviews.title: Not what you expect this watch to be
reviews.text:
There are many things with this watch that I wish I knew before I bought it and will be returning it after just 3 days of use. Here are the issues.

1. The setting for shake-awake when turned OFF does not work, it still comes on automatically
2 The Da Fit app will only send "Steps" data to Google Fit ONLY
3. To get to Strava, you have to connect Da Fit, through Google Fit, then Google Fit to Strava and you still cannot send all your data, just "Steps" (see #2)
4. The watch ASSUMES the type of activity you are doing and stores it on Da Fit app where you CANNOT adjust anything on what it just recorded to correct its assumption or delete the activity
5. The watch CANNOT act as a stand-alone Blue Tooth sensor for ANY of its features for fitness to connect directly to another fitness app like Strava
6. I took a 2.5 minute "walk" while in the shower. See #1 and #4
7. Under the Kospet website, I listed the #1 issue under their manual download area and have yet to receive a response to my post.
reviews.author: AFXABULRCRQY6TCJS7JOD3YBBTLQ

reviews.title: Update:
reviews.text:
Update: the company reached out to me and offered a refund or replacement even after the return window was closed. I chose a replacement because I really do like this watch. I haven't taken it off since I put it on and it's still working great. Outstanding customer service.

It started off great. It was easy to set up and battery lasted about 2 weeks. The heart rate, oxygen level, and blood pressure was very accurate. After about 3 weeks, the sleep monitoring stopped working. By the time I figured out it wasn't working, the return window was closed. Other than that, I love this watch and it would be an easy 5 stars if everything worked.

reviews.title: close to hitting the mark. advertising is a little off
reviews.text:
i want to rate this with 4 stars. but as of now im having issues with the watch. lets start with the pros.

Strong, great options, i love being able to check heart rate, blood pressure and sleep patterns and my rides.

Cons- I have some tech issues so far. watch is glitching out. may be a one off so im giving them a chance with a replacement well see how that one works and ill upgrade the stars that issue alone makes it 1 star as its pretty unuseable with the issue. but i get tech issues happen lets see how the next one goes.

issues that are not broken and are just part of the watch. you cant do anything when tracking an excersize. like i want to see the time when im riding my bike... cant. i want to check my heart rate... cant i want to see my blood pressure... cant.... i have to cancel the ride(tracking excersize) to see other stuff. so i end up never using it cause i want the main part of the watch... seeing what time it is. the excersize portion should run in the back ground. with out the tech issues and fixing that issue and id give it 5 stars.

few things that dont matter much you cant make calls or send texts but who cares its on a small watch i dont need that. the advertising shows that you can make calls but thats with a different watch perhaps? the language is a little... non usa english. so it feels a little off but its not so off you cant understand it. like for seven days of data or past seven days it just says," seven data." lol small issue as its a foreign watch if you're in the states so its expected. no star loss for that.

so if the tech issue is gone on the next watch and i had a one off then id say its a buy good solid watch. if not and they all have issues then its a flaw in teh design not this specific watch and its a no go ill keep you posted.
reviews.author: AFF5JA3YMHJG2K56WIFV2YNIH6UQ

reviews.title: Not a bad watch!
reviews.text: This KOSPET Tank M1 Pro is not a bad little watch! The battery life is pretty great! It is almost double the battery life of a typical fitbit! The heartrate monitoring is pretty spot on and the blood pressure measurement is a cool added feature. The BP measurement uses an algorithm to estimate your BP, which is interesting. I do question how accurate it is but it is a nice feature to track against an actual BP cuff & can be used for just in time monitoring. The screen is bright and offers lots of info right there at the flick of your wrist! The KOSPET pro comes with a few different screen protectors which is really nice! They even include a tempered glass protector! Some improvements can be made on the systems notifications front (which I reached out to support on). The KOSPET support team is extremely nice and welcoming. They are certainly welcome to updating and addressing feature requests from their users which is pretty fresh for a newer company!  I certainly recommend this startup watch for new and seasoned users! I can't wait to see what updates to the app their support team makes in the future!

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: Good watch and comfortable .
reviews.text:
Received the watch in perfect package. The app eas quick to download and essy to function. Not a fan of all face skins wish had more. Watch came with my current time and date which is always a plus not having to set that up. First week using it and only charged it once.

Some of the apps as far as blood pressure doesnt work, keeps sending a notice to wear correct. I've tighten up the bands and didn't help.

Gives notices on phone calls, messages and social media. When phone call coming in only gives you the option to end or ignore but phone will continue to ring if ignore option is pressed. You cannot reply to messages or answer phone calls from this watch.

I haved washed my truck and tested out the water resistant aspect and has not disappointed. I've dunked the watch in the bucket of soap water and wore it the entire time. No moisture shows up on the screen.

Pleased with the watch as of now especially for the price. Seen a few other brands for a bit more with the option to answer calls or reply to messages so if that an important feature then worth spending the extra amount.

reviews.title: Impressive Value at this Price Point
reviews.text: Very impressive, especially for this price.  This watch has features I frankly didn't expect.  It was pretty easy to set up and pair to my phone, with the recommended app.  The default system was metric (Celsius, 24-hr clock, etc), but I easily changed everything to English/British (fahrenheit, 12hr clock, etc).  The clock face I selected shows heart rate, date, time, local weather, steps and a couple other useful "quick-view" items.  It works very well receiving and making calls.  It has dozens of activities you can select, such as bike riding, aerobics, even pickleball, which is my favorite.  Battery life seems to be  quite good - about 10% per day, so it lasts at least a week on a charge. I'm sure that would vary by useage.  The voice assistant works nicely for simple internet queries, much like "Alexa" or "ok google."  I found the blood oxygen sensor to be accurate, but not the blood pressure feature.  Nothing against this watch, but I don't have confidence in this technology for blood pressure.  I use an FDA-certified cuff.  Having said that, I am very impressed with this smart watch.

reviews.text: Four weeks ago I purchased this watch on the recommendation of a friend. This watch is simple, has incredible battery life, has large, easy-to-read numbers (even w/o my glasses!), and I've gotten TWO WEEKS of battery life on one charge. The watch face does not tell you details (use the app to find out how your slept) and you can't reply to text messages, though the American Typewriter font used to display messages is quite legible. You can turn off the setting that lights up the watch every time you twist your wrist (so it doesn't light up when you're sleeping), set a Do Not Disturb feature and buzzes when you've reached your step goal. Use the VeryFit app to get details, set step goals, and set alarms. I have had difficulty getting alarms to work, but I'm still figuring it out. BTW I have a Google Pixel 2 phone.
